# Betti -> WASM Reference

Parent: [Gnosis Polyglot](../README.md)

The precise contract reference for the Betti -> WASM fabric: certified class
grammars, CLI flags, the `betti-wasm/1` manifest, the `scalar-v1` and
`json-v1` ABIs, the gg-walker envelope (v1/v2/v3) and cursor formats, the
BSNP session-frame spec, and the decline/divergence semantics of each lane.

Tutorial: [BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md). Mesh:
[BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md). Publishing:
[BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md).

Source anchors (all paths relative to `open-source/gnosis/polyglot` unless
absolute): Tier A `src/aot_wasm.rs`, Tier B `src/aot_wasm_c.rs`, Tier C
`src/aot_gg_embed.rs` + `gg-walker-wasm/`, CLI `src/bin/gnexec.rs`, TS
manifest validator `../src/betti-wasm-manifest.ts`, TS execution bridge
`../src/wasm-execution-bridge.ts`, BSNP codec
`../../x-gnosis/src/betti-session-codec.ts` and its Rust twin
`../../swerve/src/exec/snapshot.rs`.

## 1. Emission tiers

| Tier | Emitter | Output | Classes | `tier` | CLI surface |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | `src/aot_wasm.rs` (`emit_wasm_for_export`) | module + manifest, zero toolchain | `echo`, `fibonacci`, `fanout`, `numeric-loop`, `scalar-string` | `certified-direct` | `--emit-wasm*` flags |
| B | `src/aot_wasm_c.rs` (`compile_wasm_via_c`) | raw module bytes, no manifest | numeric-loop only | none | none (library, `examples/coverage_report.rs`, parity tests) |
| C | `src/aot_gg_embed.rs` (`prepare_gg_embed_artifacts`) + `gg-walker-wasm/` | walker module + manifest + `<artifactId>.topology.json` sidecar | `gg-embed` | `interpreter-embed` | `--print-gg-admission` only (emission is a library API) |

Dispatch order in `emit_wasm_for_export` (`aot_wasm.rs`): echo fixture,
fibonacci fixture, fanout fixture, numeric-loop, scalar-string, else fail
closed with:

```text
WASM AOT emitter supports the benchmark fixtures (echo.ts, fib.ts, fanout.gg)
and the certified numeric-loop and scalar-string classes; this topology is in
neither
```

Tier B requires a wasm32-capable clang, probed in order: `GNOSIS_WASM_CLANG`,
`/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang`, `/usr/local/opt/llvm/bin/clang`,
`clang-19`/`-18`/`-17`/`clang` — each candidate must actually compile an
empty wasm32 module to qualify (Apple system clang cannot).

Tier C ships one program-independent interpreter (`gg_walker_wasm.wasm`,
built with `panic = "abort"`); the program travels in the input envelope
(section 7). Default walker path is the crate-local release build; override
with `GG_WALKER_WASM_PATH`. The deployed fleet's copy of the walker asset
lives at `open-source/x-gnosis/src/workers/assets/gg-walker.wasm`.

## 2. CLI flags

Binary: `gnexec` (alias `monster`), `src/bin/gnexec.rs`. Invocation shape:
`monster <sourceFile> --export <fn> [flags]`.

| Flag | Default | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--export <EXPORT_NAME>` | required for emission | Which exported function to compile. |
| `--emit-wasm` | off | Emit a Tier A module instead of executing. Raw module bytes to stdout unless `--emit-wasm-out` is given. Unsupported IR fails closed (`IrNotSupported`, nonzero exit). |
| `--emit-wasm-out <PATH>` | none | Write the module to `<PATH>` and the manifest to `<PATH>` with its extension replaced by `.manifest.json` (e.g. `module.wasm` -> `module.manifest.json`). Implies `--emit-wasm`. |
| `--emit-wasm-manifest` | off | Print the `betti-wasm/1` manifest JSON to stdout. Implies `--emit-wasm`. Combining with `--emit-wasm` and no `--emit-wasm-out` is an error (both would write stdout). |
| `--print-gg-admission` | off | Print the Tier C admission report `{"admitted": bool, "reasons": [...]}` (fail-fast: at most one reason) and exit without executing. When the input file ends in `.json` it is treated as a topology sidecar and needs no language front-end. Export defaults to the last extracted topology's function name, else `app`. |

Emission takes precedence over the cached C-AOT execution path. The C-lane
siblings (`--emit-c`, `--aot-cached`, `--no-aot-c`) are out of scope here.
There is no `--emit-wasm-c` (Tier B) and no `--emit-gg-embed` (Tier C) flag.

## 3. Certified class grammars

"Accepted" means the construct compiles; everything not listed refuses at
compile time (the emitter returns nothing and the CLI fails closed).
"Traps" means the construct compiles but executes `unreachable` on the
listed condition.

### 3.1 echo, fibonacci, fanout — fixture classes

These three are recognizers for specific checked-in benchmark topologies,
not general grammars (`is_echo_fixture`, `is_fibonacci_fixture`,
`is_fanout_fixture` in `src/aot_c.rs`):

- `echo` — export named `app` calling a `greet` helper whose return
  expression is the `message` binding. ABI `json-v1`, 64 pages, no fuel.
- `fibonacci` — export named `app` calling a `fibonacci` helper. ABI
  `json-v1`, 64 pages, fueled.
- `fanout` — export named `app` containing a `FoilForkFold` node with
  `foil_lowering == "Promise.all"` and a `loadUser` callee. ABI `json-v1`,
  64 pages, no fuel. This shape is also the mesh's certified schedule class.

Programs that merely resemble these shapes but differ structurally fall
through to the general classes below or refuse.

### 3.2 numeric-loop class (`scalar-v1`)

One exported TS/JS function over f64 scalars. Parsed with the tree-sitter
TypeScript grammar — **this class (and scalar-string) is TypeScript/JavaScript
only**; other languages go through Tier C.

Entry: arity 0 or 1 (more than one parameter refuses).

Accepted statements: comments, empty statements, `let`/`const`/`var`
declarations, expression statements (assignments, `++`/`--`, compound
assignment), `return`, `if`/`else`, `while`, C-style `for`. Loops carry a
fuel decrement (initial fuel `1 << 42`); fuel exhaustion traps.

Accepted expressions:

- number literals, identifiers, `Infinity`, `NaN`, `true`/`false` (1.0/0.0),
  parenthesized expressions, unary `-` `+` `!`;
- binary `+ - * /` (native f64), comparisons `< <= > >= == === != !==`,
  short-circuit `&&` `||`;
- `%` via a helper mirroring JS semantics — **traps** unless both operands
  are integral with magnitude < 2^32;
- bitwise `& | ^` and shifts `<< >> >>>` — full-domain (see 3.5);
- fixed-size local arrays: subscript reads/writes are bounds-checked
  (**out of bounds traps**); `<array>.length` folds to a constant; any other
  member expression refuses; an array name in scalar position refuses;
- calls to user helpers defined in the same file: arity must match
  (mismatch refuses), call graph must be acyclic (**recursion refuses**);
- `Math.floor/ceil/trunc/abs/sqrt` (native instructions), `Math.round`
  (helper: half-away-from-zero, deliberately not `f64.nearest`),
  `Math.min`/`Math.max` (helpers: NaN-ignoring; zero-arg `min` is `+Infinity`,
  zero-arg `max` is `-Infinity`). Any other callee refuses.

Falling off the end of a function (implicit JS `undefined`) **traps**.

### 3.3 scalar-string class (`json-v1`)

One exported TS/JS function over ONE object parameter.

- The parameter may appear only as `input.field` member reads. Bare use of
  the parameter, nested members (`input.a.b`), and computed subscripts
  (`input[k]`) refuse.
- Statements: `let`/`const`/`var`, plain `=`, numeric `+= -= *= /= %=`,
  `++`/`--`, `return`, `if`/`else`, nested blocks. **No loops of any kind.**
- String expressions: string literals (no escape sequences; no bytes that
  would need JSON escaping), template literals, `+` concatenation where at
  least one side is statically a string.
- Numeric expressions: the numeric-loop subset minus arrays, bitwise, and
  user helper calls — literals, numeric locals/fields, `+ - * / %`,
  comparisons, `&& || !`, unary `- +`, the same `Math` subset.
- Conditions: string equality (`== === != !==`, byte compare) or numeric
  truthiness.
- No string `.length`, no indexing, no string methods, no user function
  calls.

Field typing is inferred per field, fail-closed (`FieldMode`):

| Mode | Inferred when | Runtime read | Traps when |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Num | field used numerically | strict JSON integer (optional `-`, no leading zeros, magnitude < 2^32) | `.`, `e`, `E`, non-integer, out of range |
| Strict | field compared with `==`/`===`/`!=`/`!==` (directly or via a plain alias) | JSON string required | a JSON number in the field (`42 === "42"` is false, so the module refuses to answer) |
| Poly | field used only in templates/concat | string used verbatim; integer formatted to digits | non-integral or beyond the integral f64 domain |

Number-to-string conversion answers only for integral f64 with
magnitude <= 2^53 (plus a `-` sign); non-integral formatting **traps**.
Input strings containing escapes or control bytes **trap**. Ambiguous
additions where neither side is statically a string (`input.a + input.b`,
`input.a + 1`) refuse — JS could either add or concatenate. All field reads
are hoisted to the module prologue: **a missing field traps even when
control flow would not have read it**.

### 3.4 gg-embed class (Tier C)

The property-carried GG subset the generic walker certifies
(`gg-walker-wasm/src/lib.rs`; TS admission port
`open-source/x-gnosis/src/gg-admission.ts`):

- Node labels: `Entry`/`PolyglotBridgeEntry` (optional `param_names`; the
  root frame binds at most one parameter to the program input; helper frames
  bind positionally), `Statement`/`PolyglotBridgeStatement`/
  `PolyglotBridgeCall` (callee is `Promise.all` foil fork/fold, `merge` with
  the `"|"` join, or a user helper), `Return`/`PolyglotBridgeReturn`,
  `Join`/`PolyglotBridgeJoin`, and the property-free `TsBridge*` pass-through
  family.
- `expr` forms: bare identifier, `input.<field>` (root frame only), quoted
  literal without escapes, and the extractor IR objects `literal`, `ident`,
  `member`, `template` (string-only: a non-string template part traps).
- Edges: `Process`, and `Fork`/`Fold` only as the certified foil pair.
  `Race`, `Vent`, `Interfere`, and unknown edge kinds decline at load.
- Certified branch builtins: `loadUser(userId)` -> `"<userId>:user"`,
  `loadProfile(userId)` -> `"<userId>:profile"` over a required string
  `input.userId`.
- Helpers (envelope v2): acyclic call graph (recursion declines at package
  time and traps at run time), arity checked against `param_names`, helper
  names shadowing builtins decline, helper frames cannot read root
  `input.<field>`.
- Hard bounds: `MAX_HOPS = 4096`, `MAX_CALL_DEPTH = 32`. Loop-bearing
  programs (e.g. `fib.ts`) decline by design — loops are Tier A's lane.

Admission is enforced twice: statically at package time
(`validate_program_json`, surfaced by `--print-gg-admission`) and dynamically
inside the wasm (input-dependent shapes trap).

Cross-language reach: every language extractor (`src/extractors/`:
`typescript.rs`, `python.rs`, `go.rs`, `java.rs`, `kotlin.rs`, plus the
grammar-driven `declarative.rs` covering Ruby) attaches the same IR
properties (`param_names`, `expr`, `bind`, `callee`, `args`), so the
echo-class program certifies from TypeScript, Python, Go, Ruby, and Java
sources (`tests/gg_embed_correctness.rs::gg_embed_supports_echo_class_across_languages`).

## 4. The `betti-wasm/1` manifest

Emitted pretty-printed as the sidecar, embedded verbatim as the
`gnosis-betti-manifest` custom section, validated fail-closed by
`gnosis/src/betti-wasm-manifest.ts::validateBettiWasmManifest`. Field names
are exactly as serialized (the casing is intentionally mixed):

| JSON field | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `artifact_version` | string | Always `"betti-wasm/1"`. |
| `artifactId` | string | Stable 16-hex id derived from the certified topology; the registry key. |
| `topologyHash` | string | Hash of the extracted GG topology. |
| `sourceHash` | string | Hash of the source text. |
| `language` | string | Source language (e.g. `typescript`). |
| `filePath` | string | Absolute source path at emission time (provenance only). |
| `functionName` | string | The exported source function that was compiled. |
| `certified_class` | string | `echo` \| `fibonacci` \| `fanout` \| `numeric-loop` \| `scalar-string` \| `gg-embed`. |
| `tier` | string | `certified-direct` (Tier A) \| `interpreter-embed` (Tier C). |
| `imports` | array | Always `[]` — artifacts are import-free by construction. |
| `pure` | bool | Result-cache (ESC) eligibility. |
| `limits.memoryPagesMax` | number | Instantiation memory cap in 64 KiB pages (64 for Tier A classes, 256 for gg-embed). |
| `limits.fuel` | number, optional | Initial fuel for fueled classes (fibonacci, numeric-loop); omitted otherwise. |
| `byteLength` | number | Length of the CORE module bytes, exclusive of the trailing manifest custom section. |
| `sha256` | string | SHA-256 of those same core bytes. |
| `decline_semantics` | string | `"trap"` — the runtime decline contract. |
| `wasm_opt` | bool | Whether a post-emission optimizer ran (currently `false`). |
| `export_name` | string | Entry export (`main`). |
| `abi` | string | `scalar-v1` \| `json-v1`. |
| `memory_export` | string | `memory`. |
| `alloc_export` | string | `gnosis_alloc` (json-v1). |
| `result_length_export` | string | `gnosis_result_len` (json-v1). |
| `free_export` | string | `gnosis_free` (json-v1). |

Integrity rule: `sha256`/`byteLength` cover `bytes[0..byteLength]` — the
module with the manifest section stripped — so the embedded manifest can
describe the bytes that contain it. `parseBettiManifestCustomSection` reads
the embedded copy; the emitter appends the manifest as the final section.

## 5. ABI call sequences

Reference implementation: `gnosis/src/wasm-execution-bridge.ts` (Node/edge),
mirrored by `swerve/src/exec/wasm.rs` (wasmtime). Both instantiate with zero
imports and cap memory at `limits.memoryPagesMax`.

### 5.1 scalar-v1

1. Instantiate the module (no imports).
2. Coerce each input to f64 (the bridge normalizes scalars; swerve requires
   `--input-json` to be a JSON array of numbers and checks arity against the
   export's signature).
3. Call `export_name` with the f64 arguments.
4. The single f64 return value is the result.

No memory protocol is involved; the `memory` export exists but is not read.
A trap during the call is a decline (section 9).

### 5.2 json-v1

1. Instantiate the module (no imports).
2. UTF-8 encode the input JSON; let `len` be its byte length.
3. `in_ptr = gnosis_alloc(len)`; write the bytes at `in_ptr` in the exported
   `memory`.
4. `out_ptr = main(in_ptr, len)`.
5. `out_len = gnosis_result_len()`.
6. Read `out_len` bytes at `out_ptr`; copy them out before any further calls.
7. Optionally `gnosis_free(in_ptr, len)` and `gnosis_free(out_ptr, out_len)` —
   the allocator is a monotone bump allocator and `gnosis_free` is a
   deliberate no-op (the guest owns its memory; hosts must not expect
   deallocation).
8. UTF-8 decode; an empty result decodes to `null`; otherwise `JSON.parse`.
   Output bytes are valid JSON by construction.

A trap at step 3, 4, or 5 is a decline. `gnosis_alloc` traps on non-positive
length or memory-cap exhaustion.

## 6. Envelope spec (Tier C walker input)

The gg-walker's json-v1 input is a JSON envelope. Key discipline is exact and
fail-closed: only `topology`, `helpers`, `input`, `budget`, `resume` are
allowed; any other key declines; `topology` and `input` are mandatory.

- **v1** — `{"topology": <GgTopology>, "input": <value>}`.
- **v2** — adds `"helpers": {"<name>": <GgTopology>, ...}`: user helper
  calls resolve against this map (rules in 3.4). An empty `helpers` object
  is valid v2 and equivalent to v1.
- **v3** — adds two optional keys; v1/v2 envelopes remain byte-compatible:
  - `"budget": <non-negative integer>` — a hop budget. When the walk would
    exceed it, the walker COMPLETES normally (no trap) and returns a cursor
    (section 6.1) instead of the answer.
  - `"resume": <cursor>` — a previously returned cursor; the walk re-enters
    exactly where it suspended.

Envelope version is structural — there is no version field; presence of
`helpers`/`budget`/`resume` is what distinguishes v1/v2/v3.

### 6.1 Cursor format

A suspended walk returns:

```json
{"__gg_cursor__": {
  "nodeId": "<innermost frame's node id>",
  "frames": [
    {"t": "root",           "nodeId": "...", "scope": { ... }, "pending": null},
    {"t": "helper:<name>",  "nodeId": "...", "scope": { ... }, "pending": [ ... ]}
  ],
  "pending": null,
  "ordinal": 3,
  "topo": "26b32e2d4ad4a20e"
}}
```

- `frames` is root-first; the innermost frame's node is the one ABOUT TO
  execute (suspension happens before the node runs, so resume re-enters it).
  Outer frames are in-flight helper calls. Frame `t` is `"root"` for
  `frames[0]` and `"helper:<name>"` (naming a carried helper) for the rest.
- `pending` (top-level and per-frame) holds partially-folded fork results,
  or `null`; the top-level copy must equal the innermost frame's.
- `ordinal` is total hops consumed, strictly monotone across suspensions of
  one logical walk.
- `topo` is the program fingerprint: FNV-1a 64 over the canonical
  serialization of the parsed `(topology, helpers)` pair, hex `{:016x}`.
  A cursor never resumes against a different topology — mismatch declines.

Cursor parsing is fail-closed: exactly the `__gg_cursor__` key; inner keys
exactly `nodeId|frames|pending|ordinal|topo`; non-empty frames; frame keys
exactly `t|nodeId|scope|pending`; `nodeId` and `pending` cross-checks against
the innermost frame. Every violation declines
(`gg-walker-wasm/tests/cursor_resume.rs::cursors_fail_closed`).

The certified-kind gate runs before the budget check: a node the walker
cannot classify declines exactly as it would unbounded — a cursor is never
minted pointing at an uncertified node.

## 7. BSNP frame spec (session snapshots)

BSNP ("Betti session frame") captures a wasm session for teleport/resume.
Codec: `x-gnosis/src/betti-session-codec.ts`; byte-identical Rust twin:
`swerve/src/exec/snapshot.rs` (fixture-pinned in both suites). Layout:

```text
offset  size          content
0       4             magic "BSNP" (0x42 0x53 0x4E 0x50)
4       2             version, u16 little-endian, = 1
6       4             metaLen, u32 little-endian
10      metaLen       canonical JSON metadata (UTF-8)
10+metaLen  pages*65536  linear memory image
```

Metadata fields: `artifactId`; optional `snapshotRoot` (integrity root);
`positionRoot` — a string with one of three prefixes/forms: `cursor:<...>`
(suspended at a cursor), `out:<...>` (completed with a result), or
`interrupted` (torn mid-execution — **not resumable**); `ordinal`
(monotone hop count); `globals` — the module's mutable globals, i32/i64 as
decimal strings, f32/f64 as hex bit patterns (bit-exact round-trip);
`memoryPages` — page count of the memory tail. Frames are sealed and
verified on decode; a corrupt or truncated frame refuses to decode.

Producers/consumers: `swerve cline --backend wasm --snapshot-out <path>`
writes a frame after a Completed or TimedOut run; `--resume <path>` restores
one (interrupted frames refuse). The authenticated-resume design around this
codec (capability gating, integrity roots, replay rules) is
[BETTI_STATEFUL_TELEPORT_DESIGN.md](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_STATEFUL_TELEPORT_DESIGN.md).

Verified frame head (2026-07-03): `4253 4e50 0100 f900 0000` — magic,
version 1, metaLen 249, followed by `{"artifactId":"32510684bcf0fef7",...}`.

## 8. Coverage

`scripts/coverage-report.sh` (wrapper for
`cargo run --example coverage_report`) sweeps every extractable export in
`../gnode/benchmarks/*` and asks each lane's support surface whether it
claims the export. Coverage is the union across tiers. Run on 2026-07-03:

```text
totals: 78 exports | tier A 12 | tier A/C 12 | tier B 3 | tier C 15 | any tier 21 (26%)
```

The percentage is computed at run time (it moves with the corpus and with
whether a wasm32 clang is present for Tier B probes); it is not a constant
recorded anywhere in source. The per-language "100%" tables in the crate
README measure extraction coverage, a different metric.

## 9. Decline and divergence semantics per lane

Common contract: the parity target is always the walker (the reference
interpreter). No AOT lane ever answers where it could disagree with the
walker. The decline sets of the lanes intentionally differ:

| Lane | Declines / traps | Answers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Walker (reference) | uncertified shapes decline at load; input-dependent uncertified behavior traps | everything certified |
| Tier A wasm | compile-time: outside the five classes; run-time: unparseable/escaped JSON, missing fields, fuel exhaustion, OOB indexes, implicit `undefined`, `%` outside the integral 2^32 domain, formatting beyond 2^53 | **bitwise family on the full f64 domain**: `i64.trunc_sat_f64_s` is exactly the walker's saturating cast (NaN -> 0), `f64.convert_i64_s` is exactly `as f64`, so `& \| ^ << >>` need no 2^32 fence; `>>>` via `i32.trunc_sat_f64_u` |
| Tier B (C via clang) | everything Tier A declines, PLUS the bitwise family outside the C kernel's `\|x\| < 2^32` fences (kernel-level `exit(3)` -> `__builtin_trap()`) | the rest of numeric-loop, bit-for-bit equal to Tier A where both answer |
| Tier C walker-embed | package-time: outside the property-carried subset (one fail-fast reason); run-time: uncertified input-dependent shapes, non-string template parts, hop/depth bounds | the certified GG subset, from any extractor language |

So there are domains where the wasm lane answers and the C lane declines —
by design, never the reverse direction against the walker. The cross-lane
agreements are pinned by
`tests/wasm_correctness.rs::scalar_parity_bitwise_full_domain` and
`::tier_a_and_tier_b_agree_bit_for_bit`, and Tier C by
`tests/gg_embed_correctness.rs::gg_embed_parity_vs_gnexec`.

How a trap surfaces to callers, per execution surface:

| Surface | Decline surfaces as |
| --- | --- |
| gnexec/monster emission | nonzero exit + `ir not supported` message (compile-time) |
| `GnosisWasmExecutionSurface` / harness | failed execution -> `422 {"declined": true}` at `/betti/run` |
| swerve `cline --backend wasm` | exit 126 DENIED (emitter refusal, no fallback), exit 3 (run-time trap), exit 124 (timeout), exit 1 (host error) |
| Station `/execute` | `422` |
| Mesh `/schedule` | any arm decline fails the whole schedule, `422` ([mesh guide](./BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md#5-decline-propagation)) |

## 10. Fail-closed guarantees

- Emission refuses everything outside a certified class; there is no
  best-effort compilation.
- Emitted modules have zero imports; hosts instantiate with an empty import
  object and a memory cap. Nothing ambient is reachable from the guest.
- The manifest validator refuses unknown `artifact_version`, missing fields,
  and ABI/export mismatches; stores re-verify `sha256` on load.
- Envelope and cursor parsing use exact-key discipline; unknown keys,
  malformed budgets, foreign-topology cursors all decline.
- The walker is built `panic = "abort"`: any internal error is a real trap,
  not a wrong answer.
- The certified-kind gate precedes budget suspension: cursors only ever
  point at certified nodes.
- BSNP frames are sealed; interrupted frames refuse to resume.

`polyglot/tests/wasm_correctness.rs`, `polyglot/tests/gg_embed_correctness.rs`,
`polyglot/gg-walker-wasm/tests/{walker_parity,tier_c_calls,cursor_resume}.rs`,
